source.multipart_basis: The original sample already specifies body_count=assembly with 'plating and stiffeners as sub-bodies'. Multipart decomposition formalizes the bottom shell, side shell, deck plating, longitudinal centerline girder, bottom longitudinals, and transverse frames as distinct part families.

full_prompt:
Design the SNAME CN15 Ship Design Challenge midship structural bay of a 10.0 m aluminum crew workboat as a multi-part assembly, explicitly modeling the 2.0 m-long midship bay as the physical collection of welded 5083-H116 plating panels and extruded/welded stiffeners: bottom shell, side shell, deck plating, centerline longitudinal girder, bottom longitudinals, and the two transverse frames that close the bay. The original prompt already calls out 'deliver STEP AP242 assembly of the midship bay with plating and stiffeners as sub-bodies'; this multipart sidecar formalizes that decomposition.

The assembly consists of six part families: (A) bottom shell plating (two panels, port + starboard of centerline girder), each approximately 1.6 m x 2.0 m in the midship bay; (B) side shell plating (two panels, port + starboard), each 2.0 m x 1.2 m; (C) deck plating (two panels, port + starboard of centerline), 1.6 m x 2.0 m; (D) one longitudinal centerline girder running the 2.0 m bay length (full-depth web + top/bottom flanges); (E) two bottom longitudinals at 600 mm spacing below the bottom shell running the 2.0 m bay length; (F) two transverse frames closing the bay (T-section or L-section), bracketed across the full cross-section at both ends of the 2.0 m bay.

Geometry inherits the original sample verbatim: midship bay length 2.0 m along LOA, spanning between transverse frames; hull LOA 10.0 m, beam 3.2 m, depth 1.2 m, design draft 0.70 m; longitudinal stiffener spacing 600 mm.

Material inherits verbatim: marine-grade aluminum 5083-H116 (yield 215 MPa, ultimate 305 MPa, E 70 GPa, density 2660 kg/m^3).

Loads inherit verbatim: LC1 bottom slam 68 kPa on bottom shell (ABS HSC formula at 1.5 g LCG vertical acceleration); LC2 side hydrostatic 22 kPa on side shell; LC3 deck load 6 kPa on weather deck; LC4 longitudinal hog bending 60 MPa stress at side shell.

Pass/fail criteria inherit verbatim from the original and apply at the assembly level: (R1) max von Mises under combined LC1+LC2+LC3 <= 143 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) max plating deflection under LC1 slam <= 6 mm (600 mm spacing / 100); (R3) first-mode side-shell buckling load factor under LC4 >= 2.0; (R4) intact GMt >= 0.30 m per ISO 12217-1 category B at design displacement; (R5) midship section modulus >= 3.1e-4 m^3 per ABS HSNC minimum. Added assembly-level targets under fem_check_policy.assembled_only: plating-to-stiffener weld DCR and frame-to-plating weld DCR must be reported; per-part stresses are reported for diagnostic purposes but do not gate pass/fail.

Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required; wave bending and slamming pressures are prescribed. An intact-stability hydrostatics solver (Rhino Orca3D, Maxsurf Stability) is required outside the FEA for GMt calculation. Deliver STEP AP242 of the assembly with each body (each plating panel, centerline girder, each longitudinal, each frame) as a distinct solid tagged with part_family (A..F) and instance_index, plus a scantling report listing max von Mises, plating deflection, first buckling load factor, GMt, midship section modulus, assembly mass, and weld DCR.
short_prompt:
Design the SNAME CN15 midship structural bay of a 10.0 m aluminium crew workboat as a multi-part welded assembly (bottom shell plating port+starboard, side shell plating, deck plating, centreline girder, two bottom longitudinals, two transverse frames).

Midship bay 2.0 m long; hull LOA 10.0 m, beam 3.2 m, depth 1.2 m, design draft 0.70 m; longitudinal stiffener spacing 600 mm. Material 5083-H116 (yield 215 MPa, ultimate 305 MPa, E 70 GPa, density 2660 kg/m3).

Load cases: LC1 bottom slam 68 kPa (ABS HSC 1.5 g LCG); LC2 side hydrostatic 22 kPa; LC3 deck load 6 kPa; LC4 longitudinal hog bending 60 MPa at side shell.

Pass/fail: max von Mises <= 143 MPa (yield 215 / 1.5); plating deflection <= 6 mm (600 mm / 100); first buckling factor >= 2.0; intact GMt >= 0.30 m per ISO 12217-1 category B; midship section modulus >= 3.1e-4 m3 per ABS HSNC; FEM at assembled level; weld DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: GMt and righting arm curve are closed-form from hydrostatics of the hull form, not FEA.

prompt.title: 10 m aluminum workboat midship structural bay (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Multi-part 5083-H116 midship bay: bottom shell (A) + side shell (B) + deck plating (C) + centerline girder (D) + 2 bottom longitudinals (E) + 2 transverse frames (F). 2.0 m bay length. ABS HSNC slamming 68 kPa, side 22 kPa, deck 6 kPa, hog 60 MPa. Verified at assembly level: von Mises <= 143 MPa, deflection <= 6 mm, buckling factor >= 2.0, GMt >= 0.30 m, section modulus >= 3.1e-4 m^3, weld DCR <= 1.0.

parts.name: bottom_shell_plating
parts.role: welded 5083-H116 bottom shell plating panel (port + starboard of centerline), 1.6 m x 2.0 m in the midship bay; receives LC1 slamming pressure 68 kPa

parts.name: side_shell_plating
parts.role: welded 5083-H116 side shell plating panel (port + starboard), 2.0 m x 1.2 m in the midship bay; receives LC2 hydrostatic 22 kPa and governs the LC4 longitudinal buckling check

parts.name: deck_plating
parts.role: welded 5083-H116 deck plating panel (port + starboard of centerline), 1.6 m x 2.0 m in the midship bay; receives LC3 deck load 6 kPa

parts.name: centerline_girder
parts.role: longitudinal centerline girder running the 2.0 m bay length (full-depth web + top/bottom flanges); primary hull-girder backbone in the bay

parts.name: bottom_longitudinal
parts.role: longitudinal stiffener below the bottom shell at 600 mm spacing, running the 2.0 m bay length

parts.name: transverse_frame
parts.role: transverse frame (T-section or L-section) closing the 2.0 m bay at each end; transfers slamming, hydrostatic, and deck loads from the plating into the hull girder

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 3.2 m beam x 1.2 m depth x 2.0 m bay length
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: hull coordinates: +x forward along LOA, +y starboard, +z up (deck positive)

boundary_conditions.frame_edges: transverse frames at both ends of the bay act as simple supports

objective.primary: pass all scantling and stability checks under assembled_only FEM check

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ABS HSNC minimum for L/B ratio
ABS/IACS welded-joint allowable for plating-to-stiffener and frame-to-plating fillet welds

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for plating, beam for stiffeners

interfaces.load_path: continuous fillet weld between bottom shell plating and bottom longitudinals; transfers slamming pressure into stiffeners
fillet weld between bottom shell and each transverse frame; closes the bay bottom load path
fillet weld between side shell and each transverse frame; transfers hydrostatic pressure into the frames
fillet weld between deck plating and each transverse frame; transfers deck load + hog bending into the frames
fillet weld between bottom shell and centerline girder; splits the bottom slamming load between port and starboard
centerline girder end-connects to each transverse frame via welded gusset plate

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Welded aluminum workboat hulls are assemblies of plating panels and stiffeners; the multipart sidecar formalizes the bottom/side/deck plating + centerline girder + longitudinals + transverse frames decomposition.
notes.speculation_caveats: Exact stiffener gauge/section is team-selected per ABS HSNC scantling rules; the original sample prescribes only spacing (600 mm) and overall dimensions.
